title: daucus carota subsp. halophilus
intro: Daucus carota subsp. halophilus, also known as the halophilous wild carrot, is a subspecies of wild carrot that is adapted to grow in salt-rich environments. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Daucus carota subsp. halophilus include its potential to protect against oxidative stress, reduce the risk of cancer, and support the digestive system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and health.
